prompt:
A medium-shot authentic daily life photograph of an East Asian young woman standing beside a scholar's desk in a dim interior study, natural candid moment framing. 85mm lens, waist-up, shallow depth of field. The background: dark bookshelves receding into soft darkness, a single inkstone on the desk surface catching reflected window light.
East Asian young woman in her early 20s. Almond-shaped eyes with soft single eyelids, composed and slightly downcast. Delicate refined nose bridge. Skin fair to light beige, smooth in even diffused light. Skin subsurface scattering visible under directional light, specular micro-highlights on cheekbones and nose ridge, fine foundation powder grain perceptible.
She wears a deep navy silk qipao with interlocking vine embroidery in faded slate-grey thread — historical pattern, slightly muted, the navy absorbed into aged silk like old ink. Mandarin collar, short sleeves, clean and fitted. One hand rests on the edge of the desk near the inkstone, fingers just touching the dark stone without purpose. The room exists at the edge of what natural light can reach. Two or three stray hairs displaced by faint air movement, natural unplanned imperfection, not geometrically symmetrical.
Overcast afternoon light filters through a small high window off-frame, arriving as a soft ambient wash with no strong directionality — cool and grey, making the deep navy appear almost black against pale walls. Silence of concentration without a task. Subtle ISO 400 film grain in shadow areas, photographic noise texture not CG render smoothness. Aspect ratio 9:16. No watermark, no text overlay, not cartoon, not digitally painted, not illustration, not anime.
